calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Denise Weeks To Enforce Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-16-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Denise Weeks To Enforce Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar May-22-2018 Continued To Aug-15-18 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-09-2018 Vacated

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jan-29-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Howard Slavitt & Fredrick Crombie for the Coblentz Firm. Michael Freedman for Denise Weeks. William Weeks also present.

Mr. Freedman reported that Ms. Weeks has been found, that she has signed the Settlement Agreement (SA) that it has been provided to counsel and that now counsel wants to decline the SA. Mr. Slavitt reported that Mr. Weeks has been informed in regards to their withdrawal of counsel, that they received the signed SA but changes had been made, that there are concerns regarding the side letters and that they would need to reconcile the forms of the SA. The Court asked if this could be worked out before they withdraw. Mr. Slavitt stated it could not and that the form they agreed to sign on 10/5 is not the form that Ms. Weeks signed. Mr. Freedman explained that they did not make any changes after they agreed on the draft, that they only incorporated the agreed upon sentence into this form as they could not find the corrected draft for signing. The Court asked counsel to step out and review the SA to try for an agreement now. The matter was recessed for counsel to confer. Upon return Mr. Slavitt reported that they worked out the difference, that they have the signature page and will have all of the other by this afternoon, that they will forward them to Mr. Freedmna and that the SA will be final. Mr. Slavitt further stated that per Rule 3.700 they will continue to exchange settlement documents. The Court granted the Motion to be Relieved as Counsel in open court. The status of mediation is off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Nuque. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Aug-04-2017 Continued To Feb-13-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Continued To Oct-30-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Continued To Oct-04-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Michael Freedman for Denise Weeks.
Fredrick Crombie for William Weeks (Trustee).

Mr. Freedman stated that the Attorneys spent time working with the Clients and had a draft of an agreement, that he believes opposing Counsel's Client has signed it, that his Client is under a tremendous amount of stress, and spoke of his Client's disability and medical condition. Mr. Freedman also stated that his Client was homeless at some point, that she hasn't been able to get in touch with him, and that he has ben trying to reach her by her cell phone and e-mail, but got no response. In addition, Mr. Freedman stated that he got a hold of the person/Friend whom she was previously living with, that this Friend offered to go look for her and asked for several more weeks stating that they are at the point of settlement. Mr. Crombie stated that there is cell phone that the Trustee has been paying for. Status of mediation is continued to October 4, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Jul-12-2017 Continued To Sep-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frederick Crombie for William Weeks (Trustee).
Michael Freedman for Denise Weeks (Respondent).

Mr. Freedman reported that they have made progress, that a couple of major issues were resolved in the drafting process and that he thinks they have a final version of the settlement. Mr. Freedman also stated that his Client has to go over it and discuss the final draft with him, that he hopes to have a response and answer from his Client within a day or two and then have the Trustee file a petition for approval of the settlement. Status on mediation is continued to September 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 with the hope that this can go off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Jun-12-2017 Continued To Jul-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Howard Slavitt for petitioner William Weeks; Michael Freedman for respondent Denise Weeks.

Mr. Freedman reported that there had been a mediation in April. He said that there are still some issues they are working to resolve, and that he expected a settlement agreement will be reached. Mr. Slavitt agreed. The matter was continued to July 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Court Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar May-01-2017 Continued To Jun-12-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Howard Slavitt for William Weeks (Trustee).
Michael Freedman for Denise Weeks (Respondent).

Mr. Slavitt stated that mediation was generally successful, that they have an agreement in principal and that they agreed to a four continuance to document the agreement. Status of mediation is continued to June 12, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

Petition For Approval Of Inventory, For Instruction, And For An Order Concercening The Trustee'S Cla
Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Continued To Oct-23-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Howard Slavitt for Williams Weeks (Trustee).
Michael Freedman for and with Denise Weeks (Respondent).

Mr. Freedman stated that he filed a substitution of attorney on Friday, that he had productive conversation with Mr. Slavitt, that the matter qualifies for pro bono mediation, that the issue regards rents and that they can do mediation within 30 to 60 days if they can get an accounting. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. The Petition for Approval of Inventory, for Instructions, etc. was continued to and the Parties are to return on May 1, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Court also signed an order/notice of trial setting the matter for trial in the department of the Presiding Judge (206) on October 23, 2017 at 9:30 a.m. Endorsed copies of both orders were provided to Counsel in open Court.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Reporter: Joan Marie Columbini, CSR #5435, 415-597-5600.

Petition For Approval Of Inventory, For Instruction, And For An Order Concercening The Trustee'S Cla
Off Calendar Jan-23-2017 Continued To Feb-27-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Fred Crombie for and with William Weeks.
Denise Weeks (Beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Weeks stated that she has a contract with an Attorney and that she is hoping to know something this afternoon. Mr. Crombie spoke of Ms. Weeks delay pattern, stated that they scheduled mediation dates and Ms. Weeks would not agree to any of them, that there is outstanding discovery and suggested setting the matter for trial. Mr. Crombie also stated that there are issues concerning loans to Renee Weeks, compensation to Denise for providing care, a writ of possession regarding the house which is up for execution, that he intends to do a motion for summary judgment, and that they made multiple attempts to provide funds due to Ms. Weeks. Ms. Week also stated that she objects to everything, spoke of dealings with her prior attorney and stated that she has an issue with how the money is being characterized. The Petition for Approval of Inventory, for Instruction, etc. is continued to February 27, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If there is no Attorney for Ms. Weeks on that day, the Court will set the matter for trial and set a mediation date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jan-11-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Konstantine Demiris for Denise Weeks.
Fredrick Crombie appeared by telephone for William Weeks (Trustee).

Mr. Demiris stated that he did not receive opposition from his Client, that his Client hired another Attorney but has refused to provide information and any communication, and that the Attorney stated that he is not stepping into this action. The Court granted the Motion for Withdrawal of Attorney of Record (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Chien. Not reported.

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Dec-01-2016 Continued To Jan-09-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Inventory, For Instruction, And For An Order Concercening The Trustee'S Cla
Off Calendar Dec-05-2016 Continued To Jan-23-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Inventory, For Instruction, And For An Order Concercening The Trustee'S Cla
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Continued To Dec-05-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Fred Crombie for and with (petitioner) William Weeks. Konstantine Demiris (via Court Call) for Denise Weeks.

The Court stated that at the last hearing co-counsel of Mr. Demiris appeared and that there was some misunderstanding. Mr. Demiris stated that he and Mr. Crombie were in discussions regarding the preemptory challenge of Judge Busch, that Mr. Moore had difficulty hearing and thought that Judge Cheng was Judge Busch, that they are not trying to delay and request that this be set for pro bono mediation. The Court stated that there has been no sworn declaration and that Mr. Moore was not sworn in and asked if we can get beyond the misunderstanding. Mr. Crombie stated that they object to mediation, that over a year ago they had reached an agreement regarding private mediation and had a date scheduled which was cancelled by Ms. Weeks, that they have spent many hours trying to get this to mediation, that Ms. Weeks opted not to do it, that they are now pushing for pro bono mediation is a delay tactic, we do not agree to mediation at this time and that there is no hope of getting this resolved. Mr. Demiris stated that Mr. Crombie is not accurate, that prior counsel and I picked Tom Cain as a mediator, that there were scheduling issues on both sides, that unilateral confirmations did not work, that there was 3 months of silence ending with the attorney being fired and Mr. Crombie being hired, that pro bono mediation will eliminate costs, that Mr. Crombie is not willing, that Ms. Weeks was always willing to mediate and that counsel's arguments do not make sense. The Court stated that it is hearing that there is a commitment to mediation and that the goal is to get mediation done. Mr. Crombie stated that there is a separate Unlawful Detainer matter in San Mateo County, that they expect to be in trial in October, that there will be a mandatory settlement conference, the proceeding is to evict Ms. Weeks and that they do not think there is a need for pro bono mediation in this case. The Court asked if there would be a global settlement in San Mateo, that regarding the 170.6 challenge it will set aside the ruling as no declaration was filed and that Mr. Moore was not sworn in, that in regards to mediation it will set a deadline of November 28, 2016 and if the Unlawful Detainer is not successful they are to proceed to mediation. Mr. Crombie stated that they are open to Judge Eaton. Mr. Demiris stated that they had suggested him and that his client is happy to mediate with Judge Eaton if they will not agree to pro bono mediation. Mr. Crombie stated that he does not want to see this stayed, that they want to continue to move forward, and that they should start preparing for trial. The Court suggested March or April 2017 for trial. Mr. Demiris stated that he believes that mediation will resolve the issues. The Court continued the Petition for Approval of Inventory, etc., to December 5, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the parties are to notify the Court as to the status on mediation. Mr. Crombie stated that it is imperative to set a trial date and that it is the only thing that will keep this moving forward. The Court stated that it will not set a trial date today but will set it on December 5, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. Court Reporter: Kayla M. Knowles, CSR #14071, 916-880-0971 (Reported).

Petition For Approval Of Inventory, For Instruction, And For An Order Concercening The Trustee'S Cla
Off Calendar Sep-20-2016 Continued To Sep-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Inventory, For Instruction, And For An Order Concercening The Trustee'S Cla
Off Calendar Aug-10-2016 Continued To Sep-20-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Frederick Crombie for and with (trustee) William Weeks. Christopher Moore (via court call) for (beneficiary) Denise Weeks.

The Court addressed Mr. Moore stating that it received a communication regarding logistical issues and asked if they object to the petition. Mr. Moore confirmed that they object, that they would also like to make a peremptory challenge and that they would like the matter continued for 60 days due to the non-availability of Mr. Demiris. Mr. Crombie stated that they object to the delay, that this petition has been on file for two months and during that time no objections were made, that all of the issues to resolve are with Ms. Weeks and that to go out for 4 months is concerning. Mr. Moore stated that Mr. Demiris represents that a continuance had been discussed, that there have been efforts to mediate, that a mediator had been agreed upon but was let go and that the non-availability of Mr. Demiris is not a surprise. Mr. Crombie responded regarding the mediation stating that they had tried and had a date set, that Ms. Weeks cancelled but did not agree to a new date, that they have offered a settlement with no response and that opposing counsel's strategy is to delay to keep her in the property which is preventing distribution. The Petition for Approval of Inventory, for Instruction and for an Order Concerning the Trustee's CLA was continued to September 20, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard by Judge Busch. Mr. Crombie requested that the Court set a deadline for opposing counsel to file their objection. The Court asked Mr. Moore when the written objection could be filed. Mr. Moore responded that they could not do it until the end of September. Mr. Crombie responded that they have already had two months to do so. The Court set the deadline of September 6, 2016 for filing the objection.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. Court Reporter: Joan Marie Columbini, CSR #5435, 415-597-5600 (Reported)
